subject: Public Health
title: Social Determinants of Health
description: A fast breakdown of Social Determinants of Health for builders.

hook.text: Your zip code predicts your health better than your genetic code.

buildup.text: Income, education, housing, and employment shape health outcomes more than medical care does.

discovery.text: People in poor neighborhoods die years earlier—even in wealthy countries with universal healthcare.

twist.text: Medical care accounts for only about 20% of health outcomes. Social factors drive the rest.

climax.text: Addressing social determinants means tackling poverty, racism, and inequality—not just disease.

punchline.text: Health starts where you live, work, and play.

quiz.question: Roughly what percentage of health outcomes does medical care account for?
